topic: A student learns their scholarship application was denied
raw_output:
[story 1]

The rejection letter lay crumpled on the desk, a flimsy piece of paper mocking the hours I’d poured into that application. My hands were shaking so badly I could barely hold the pen, and when I finally managed to smooth the crease, the stark 'Denied' burned into my vision. I slammed my fist onto the wooden surface, the sound echoing sharply in the quiet apartment. Every nerve ending felt tight, coiled with a desperate, hot energy. I stood up, pacing the small room like a caged animal, kicking at the rug. Why? What did I do wrong? The sheer audacity of it made my jaw clench so hard I tasted copper. I wanted to rip the letter to shreds, to burn the entire submission process to the ground.

[story 2]

"Unbelievable," I muttered, the word scraping out of my throat like broken glass. I stared at the screen, the official notification glowing mockingly on my monitor. It was a cold, definitive statement, a wall slammed shut in my face. A wave of intense heat washed over me, starting in my chest and spreading outward, making my breath catch in ragged gasps. I gripped the edge of the desk until my knuckles were white, the strain visible in the cords of my neck. I walked to the window, staring out at the indifferent city skyline, feeling a profound sense of injustice settle over me. This wasn't just a setback; it felt like a personal affront, a deliberate slight against everything I’d worked for. I turned back, my eyes blazing with a sharp, unyielding intensity.

[story 3]

I reread the rejection email for the tenth time, my eyes scanning the carefully chosen, sterile language as if searching for a hidden flaw, a typo that would expose the whole thing as fraudulent. A low, guttural sound escaped me—a sound of pure, contained agitation. I paced the length of the hallway, my footsteps heavy and deliberate, each stride fueled by a simmering resentment. My shirt felt too tight, constricting my movement. I stopped abruptly in front of my mirror, studying my reflection. The anger wasn't just frustration; it was a deep, churning pressure building behind my ribs, demanding an outlet. I clenched my fists until my nails dug crescents into my palms, the effort making my muscles tremble violently. I needed to dismantle this outcome, right now.
